pages.full_text:
outside the farm was moorland. Thomas got badly shocked by an electric fence. Charles saw a black sheep & said "look, mummy there's a sheep all covered in pooh!"

[sketch of building/farm structure]

The wall are interesting here - stone walls with beech trees growing out of the top of them. #
		It has become cold with the sun going in.
		John was feeding ducks & said "Mummy, why don't they make ducks with fur?"

__Sat 4th Sept__
		In the morning it was sunny again & we explored __Dunster__, but didn't have time to see the Castle. John got his feet wet in the mill race, & we saw a beautiful pack-horse bridge, meadows, hills, paths & a thatched cottage. Several thatched houses.
pages.route_sketch_description: Small hand-drawn sketch of what appears to be a farm building or structure, possibly depicting the moorland farm mentioned in the text. No place labels or route arrows visible.
pages.notes: Page begins mid-paragraph, continuing from the previous spread. No handwritten page number visible at top-left — the page may be unnumbered or the number is not in frame. The date 'Sat 4th Sept' with no year given; within volume A (1989–2003) a Saturday 4th September could fit 1993, 1999 or other years — cannot confirm from page content alone. The sketch is small and schematic, likely illustrating the farm setting described in the text.
